name: Mem0
maintainer: Mem0 AI Inc. (YC-backed, $24M Series A October 2025)
activity: ~53,500 GitHub stars (up from ~48,700 in early 2026), ~6,000 forks, 312+ total releases, multiple releases per week. High contributor activity. Open issues count not confirmed precisely.
storage_backend: multiple configurable — vector stores: Qdrant (default), Chroma, Pinecone, Redis, OpenSearch, PGVector, Milvus, Azure AI Search, Elasticsearch; graph stores (optional Mem0g): Neo4j (default), Memgraph, Neptune, Kuzu, Apache AGE, FalkorDB; history/metadata: SQLite or Postgres
representation_primitive: dual-store: semantic memory facts stored as LLM-extracted text snippets embedded in vector store + optional entity-relationship triples in KG layer (Mem0g). Each memory is a distilled factual string. Graph variant adds entity nodes with type labels and directed typed relationship edges. Three scoping levels: user, session, agent.
api_shape: Python library (pip install mem0ai) + REST API (self-hosted Docker or managed cloud) + first-party MCP server (mem0-mcp v0.2.1 PyPI December 2025; Mem0 Plugin for AI Editors with 9 MCP tools launched March-April 2026; standalone GitHub repo archived April 2026)
hosting: both — self-host via Docker (OSS) or managed cloud at app.mem0.ai; free tier available on Platform
llm_dependency: ingest primarily — LLM required at add() for fact extraction (single-pass ADD-only call); default gpt-4o-mini. Graph variant (Mem0g) adds one LLM call for entity/relation extraction at ingest. Retrieval does not require LLM (vector + BM25 + entity score fusion). LLM swappable to Anthropic Claude, Ollama, Groq, Together, Gemini via config.
schema_extensibility: LLM-learns (open discovery) — entity types and relationship labels inferred by LLM at extraction time; no fixed schema enforced in OSS. Platform tier supports custom categories and extraction instructions to guide LLM toward desired types.
schema_richness: Open/unconstrained in OSS: LLM generates entity types and edge labels from conversation context. Examples: Person, Location, Event, Preference nodes; PREFERS, AVOIDS, CONTAINS, BELONGS_TO edges. No fixed count of entity or edge types. Far from Gimle §5's prescribed 20-entity/15-edge schema. Mem0g (graph layer) is Neo4j-backed in OSS but Pro-tier only on managed API. Multi-label: not documented. Multi-graph: supported via user_id/agent_id/run_id/project_id/org_id namespace fields.
temporal_model: soft-deletion snapshot — graph relationships can be marked invalid (soft delete) to preserve historical context. Memory records have created_at timestamps. No bi-temporal valid_from/valid_to or recorded_at. Platform-only: memory expiration by date. Contrast: Zep/Graphiti provides full bi-temporal model.
forgetting_policy: semantic-dedup + LLM-driven decay — new algorithm (April 2026) is ADD-only (no explicit DELETE/UPDATE LLM calls); relevance scoring inspired by Ebbinghaus forgetting curve; Platform supports explicit expiration dates; low-relevance entries decay over time
ingest_latency_class: real-time-incremental — single LLM extraction call per add() + batch embed + batch insert; benchmark p50 latency 0.88–1.09s (LoCoMo/LongMemEval)
retrieval_modes: hybrid — parallel scoring: (1) vector similarity search, (2) BM25 keyword matching, (3) entity graph boost (entity linking from query to KG nodes); weighted score fusion; top-K selection. Graph variant adds graph traversal for relational queries.
retrieval_agenticity: static-lookup with multi-signal fusion — search() is a direct call (no HyDE, Self-RAG loop, or CRAG); multi-signal scoring improves recall without agent reasoning loop; no explicit multi-hop traversal in base OSS
retrieval_budget_control: no — no first-class per-query token/hop budget parameters; token efficiency improved algorithmically (avg <7K tokens per retrieval call in new algorithm, April 2026)
multi_project_namespace: project-label / per-agent-namespace — data partitioned via user_id, agent_id, run_id, project_id, org_id fields on each memory record. Cross-project queries not explicitly supported (filters are AND conditions). Known bug (July 2025 issue #3218): combined user_id + agent_id filter returns no results in some versions.
multi_user_access: per-agent-namespace — namespacing via user_id/agent_id/run_id; no RBAC in OSS; Platform adds org_id, project_id for team isolation
embedder_options: built-in adapters: OpenAI (default: text-embedding-3-small, 1536 dims), Ollama (nomic-embed-text 768 dims, mxbai-embed-large), Azure OpenAI, Gemini, Vertex AI, HuggingFace, Together, LangChain. Custom embedder via config. Known bug: Ollama + Qdrant defaults to 1536 dims instead of model-actual dims.
llm_client_options: OpenAI (default gpt-4o-mini), Anthropic Claude, Ollama, Groq, Together, Mistral, Google Gemini, AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, LiteLLM proxy. LLM cannot be disabled — required for fact extraction at ingest. No structured-writes-only mode.
cost_privacy: Token cost: avg <7,000 tokens per retrieval call (new algorithm April 2026); single LLM extraction call per add() episode; ~90% cost reduction vs naive approaches (self-reported). Cloud deployment sends data to Mem0's servers. Self-hosted OSS keeps data on-premise; LLM calls go to external provider unless Ollama used locally.
code_artifact_linkage: none natively — Mem0 extracts semantic facts from conversational/document text; no first-party code entity types (File, Symbol, Function, PR, Commit). Code snippets can be ingested as text but no structured code graph or AST linkage is supported.
code_analysis_features: none — no call graph, symbol index, diff-awareness, import resolution, or AST parsing; code understanding requires external preprocessing before ingestion
memory_os_layer: plaintext — semantic fact strings in vector store, optional KG for entities; no parametric (weight-tuning) or activation (KV-cache) layer
self_improvement_mechanism: LLM-reflection (partial) — ADD-only single-pass extraction reduces drift vs prior UPDATE/DELETE approach; Ebbinghaus-inspired relevance scoring for decay; Platform adds memory consolidation. No explicit feedback loop from execution outcomes to schema or extraction quality.
provenance_standard: custom-log — memory records carry id, created_at, updated_at, hash; no W3C-PROV or OpenTelemetry out-of-box
benchmark_results: Self-reported (April 2026, new algorithm): LoCoMo 91.6 (+20 pts vs prior Mem0), LongMemEval 93.4 (+26 pts vs prior; single-session assistant +53.6, temporal reasoning +42.1, knowledge updates +16.7), BEAM-1M 64.1, BEAM-10M 48.6. Avg <7K tokens/retrieval. LiCoMemory (Nov 2025, GPT-4o-mini backbone) scored 73.8% accuracy on LongMemEval — Mem0's new algorithm (93.4) leads on this benchmark using different backbone. Cross-backbone comparison caveat applies.
strengths: 1. Best-in-class published LongMemEval score (93.4, April 2026) combined with token efficiency (<7K tokens/call, ~90% cost reduction) — directly addresses Gimle's retrieval quality and cost constraints. 2. Mature multi-signal retrieval (vector + BM25 + entity graph boost) with proven production scale; easy Python/MCP integration for Gimle's agent pipeline. 3. Flexible multi-namespace partitioning (user_id/agent_id/run_id/project_id/org_id) maps well to Gimle's multi-agent team architecture.
weaknesses: 1. No bi-temporal model — Gimle §5 requires valid_from/valid_to + recorded_at; Mem0 has only created_at with soft-delete; temporal reasoning is approximated not structurally encoded. 2. Open/unconstrained schema — Gimle needs a prescribed 20-entity/15-edge domain schema; Mem0's LLM-open extraction produces inconsistent types across episodes without heavy prompt engineering. 3. Graph memory (Mem0g) is Pro-tier on managed API; OSS Neo4j graph memory has known filter bugs and requires separate Neo4j infrastructure.
fit_with_gimle_section_5: Verdict: steal-specific-ideas — not adopt-whole due to absent bi-temporal model and unconstrained schema. Key ideas to steal: (1) Multi-signal retrieval fusion (vector + BM25 + entity-boost): Gimle's retrieval layer should combine all three rather than vector-only. Benchmarked fusion achieves 93.4 on LongMemEval. (2) ADD-only single-pass LLM extraction: avoids UPDATE/DELETE complexity; Gimle's ingest pipeline can extract only new facts per episode, letting the bi-temporal layer handle supersession via valid_to timestamps rather than in-place mutation. (3) Namespace hierarchy design (user_id/agent_id/run_id/project_id/org_id): Gimle's group_id + project label can be extended — agent_id as first-class field on memory nodes aligns with Gimle's paperclip agent team model. Schema contrast: Mem0's open extraction produces ephemeral LLM-determined types (PREFERS, AVOIDS) with no analogue to Gimle's prescribed Project/Module/Symbol/Decision nodes. Gimle needs OntoKG-style routing discipline at the domain level. Not adopt-whole: missing bi-temporal model (critical blocker for Gimle §5), no code artifact linkage, schema too open for Gimle's structured domain.
